mission.title: Install and Configure llm-d
mission.description: llm-d is a high-performance distributed inference serving stack optimized for Kubernetes, designed to achieve state-of-the-art inference performance with modern accelerators. It is ideal for production deployments requiring efficient orchestration of large language models. Key trade-offs include complexity in setup and the need for adequate resources to leverage its full capabilities.

steps.title: Install the CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the llm-d command line interface, which is necessary for managing deployments.

```bash

curl -LO https://github.com/llm-d/llm-d/releases/download/v0.6.0/llm-d-linux-amd64.tar.gz

tar -xzf llm-d-linux-amd64.tar.gz

sudo mv llm-d /usr/local/bin/

llm-d version

```

> Download the latest CLI binary.
> Extract the downloaded tarball.
> Move the binary to a directory in your PATH.
> Verify the installation by checking the version.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a managed Kubernetes cluster with the specified version and node count.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create llm-d-cluster --cluster-version=1.31 --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1

```

> Create a cluster with 3 nodes in the specified region.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your kubeconfig to interact with the newly created cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials llm-d-cluster --region=us-central1

```

> Fetch the credentials for the cluster.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This step checks that the nodes in the cluster are ready and running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> List all nodes and their statuses.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step sets up autoscaling and monitoring for your llm-d deployment.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/llm-d/llm-d/main/deploy/autoscaling.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/llm-d/llm-d/main/deploy/monitoring.yaml

```

> Apply the autoscaling configuration.
> Apply the monitoring configuration.

uninstall.title: Uninstall llm-d
uninstall.description:
This step removes the llm-d deployment from your cluster. Be aware that this will result in data loss and orphaned resources.

```bash

kubectl delete deployment llm-d --namespace=default

gcloud container clusters delete llm-d-cluster --region=us-central1 --quiet

```

> Delete the llm-d deployment.
> Delete the entire cluster and associated resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade llm-d
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your configurations and data. This step includes draining nodes if necessary.

```bash

kubectl drain <node-name> --ignore-daemonsets

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/llm-d/llm-d/main/deploy/llm-d-v0.6.0.yaml

kubectl uncordon <node-name>

kubectl get pods -n default

```

> Drain the node to prepare for upgrade.
> Apply the new version of the deployment.
> Uncordon the node after upgrade.
> Verify that all pods are running.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to pull image'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified image may not exist or the registry is unreachable.

**Fix:**
Check the image name and ensure the registry is accessible.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster does not have enough resources allocated for the deployment.

**Fix:**
Increase the node size or number of nodes in the cluster.

troubleshooting.title: Pods are in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The application may be misconfigured or missing dependencies.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name>' for more details.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Connection refused'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The service may not be properly exposed or the deployment is not running.

**Fix:**
Ensure the service is correctly configured and the deployment is healthy.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new inference scheduling algorithms and improved autoscaling capabilities.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: Update your configurations to use the new scheduling algorithms.

resolution.summary: You should see output from 'kubectl get pods' indicating that all pods are running and ready.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and resources before starting the installation.
Ensure you have the necessary tools and resources before proceeding with the installation.
